Container.Name: UnrarTool
Container.Overview:
UnrarTool — a sleek self-hosted RAR extraction tool built for Unraid.
Automatically detects and extracts split RAR archives (RAR5, multi-part .partNN.rar and legacy .r00/.r01 formats) downloaded by Sonarr, Radarr, or any other downloader.
Features: ▸ File Browser — navigate your media folders and queue any folder manually with one click ▸ Real-time progress — smooth progress bar driven directly from unrar's output ▸ Job Queue — live ETA for every extraction job ▸ Watch Folders — filesystem events trigger extraction the moment a download finishes ▸ Scheduler — periodic background scan of all watch folders ▸ Smart Exclusions — auto-excludes completed archives so they are NEVER re-extracted; manual "Mark as Done" button always visible in the file browser ▸ Force Re-extract — override any exclusion when you need to re-run ▸ Password support — per-folder RAR passwords for encrypted archives ▸ Dark / Light theme toggle ▸ Full filterable log viewer
Container.Description: UnrarTool — Automated split-RAR extractor with a modern web UI. Real-time progress, smart exclusions, watch folders, and manual file browser. Built for Sonarr/Radarr workflows on Unraid.
Container.Changes: ### 1.0.0 - Initial release - File browser with always-visible Extract and Mark as Done buttons - Real-time progress bar driven from unrar stdout - Watch folders with filesystem event detection - Automatic exclusion after successful extraction - Manual Mark as Done button per folder - Force Re-extract to override any exclusion - Dark/Light theme - Full filterable log viewer - Per-folder password support for encrypted RARs

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port for the UnrarTool web interface. Change the left number to remap to a different host port.

Config.Name: Config and Database
Config.Description: Stores the SQLite database, settings, and trash folder. Must be persistent across container restarts.

Config.Name: Downloads / Media Root
Config.Description: Root path shown in the file browser. Mount your downloads or media folder here. You can add more volume paths by clicking Add another Path in the Unraid Docker settings.

Config.Name: Media Library (optional)
Config.Description: Optional: mount a second location such as your finished media library so you can browse and manage it from the same UI.

Config.Name: Data Path
Config.Description: The root path shown in the file browser on first load. Should match the container-side mount point of your Downloads volume above.

Config.Name: Config Path
Config.Description: Where the SQLite database and app settings are stored. Must match the Config volume above.

Config.Name: Web UI Port (internal)
Config.Description: Internal port the web server listens on. Must match the Port mapping above. Only change this if you have a conflict.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID the container runs as. Set to your Unraid user ID (99 for nobody) so extracted files are owned correctly.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID the container runs as. Set to your Unraid group ID (100 for users).
